title: Earnings: As of May 31, 2026
content:
Net Income for the credit union was $265,377 in Q2'2026.

The primary earnings driver this period was Net Interest Income. With Interest Income at $1,178,992 and Interest Expense of $393,101, the resulting spread contributed significantly to profitability for the quarter.

Non-Interest Income totaled $61,283 during the period. The largest sub-component was Fee Income ($560,366), followed by Other Income ($44,516) and Gain from Bargain Purchase of Assets ($28,579). While present, this category served as a modest secondary contributor to overall earnings compared to core spread income.

Non-Interest Expense was $560,366 for the quarter. The expense base is predominantly concentrated in Employee Compensation (implied by context and magnitude relative to other categories), followed by Office Occupancy ($87,671) and Loan Servicing Expenses ($24,498). Overall expense levels appear aligned with the revenue generated.

Overall Position: Net income of $265,377 was produced when strong spread earnings were offset by non-interest expenses. Sustaining profitability will depend on maintaining this interest spread while managing the concentrated employee compensation base.
